source_domains: Charset, collation, safe-collation checks, and invalid text stripping
Table lists, prefixes, blog switching, and multisite table state
Database bootstrap, connection, server capability, and unavailable database boundaries

cases.focus: DB_CHARSET/DB_COLLATE bootstrap constants and charset/collate string rendering
no-mysqli boundary where charset/collate inputs are preserved
server version parsing and capability switches without a live mysqli handle
set_charset collation-capability guard before native mysqli calls
SHOW FULL COLUMNS metadata interpretation, binary/ascii/latin1 handling, caching, and pre_get_table_charset filter short-circuit
primed column charset and length lookup from table metadata
ASCII fast path, non-ASCII table extraction, safe/unsafe collation lists, latin1 shortcut, and missing-table failure
table name parsing across SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL/SHOW forms
field type bootstrap, base/blog prefix assignment, multisite blog switching, and custom user table constants
invalid prefix WP_Error and blog prefix matrix for single-site versus multisite scenarios
all/blog/global/ms_global/old table scopes with and without prefixes
host, port, socket, IPv4, IPv6, bracketed IPv6, and invalid host parsing
deprecated facade event and has_cap('collation') delegation

native_boundaries.reason: wpdb::set_charset() calls mysqli_set_charset() and mysqli_query() only when a live high-capability mysqli handle exists. The fixture records the pre-native no-op guard and leaves live charset negotiation to the DB runtime gate.

wpdb::get_table_charset(), get_col_charset(), get_col_length(), and check_safe_collation() normally depend on SHOW FULL COLUMNS. The probe supplies deterministic column metadata while preserving WordPress metadata interpretation logic.

MULTISITE, DB_CHARSET, DB_COLLATE, CUSTOM_USER_TABLE, and CUSTOM_USER_META_TABLE are PHP constants. The fixture runs separate PHP processes for single-site and multisite scenarios so bootstrap behavior is recorded without redefining constants.
wpdb::has_cap() and db_version() are exercised through an overridden db_server_info() provider so capability logic is covered without a live database connection.

value.value: DEFAULT CHARACTER SET utf8 COLLATE utf8_general_ci
no live mysqli handle: determine_charset returns the requested charset/collate pair
high-capability set_charset path calls mysqli_set_charset()/mysqli_query() and is deferred to the live DB gate
DEFAULT CHARACTER SET utf8 COLLATE utf8_general_ci
no live mysqli handle: determine_charset returns the requested charset/collate pair
high-capability set_charset path calls mysqli_set_charset()/mysqli_query() and is deferred to the live DB gate

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate side is a copied WordPress oracle source tree until charset/collation and prefix helpers are promoted behind typed Haxe parity candidates.
The fixture avoids real MySQL handles. Native mysqli_set_charset(), SET NAMES execution, and server-reported charset behavior need the database runtime integration harness.
SHOW FULL COLUMNS output is deterministic fixture data here. Real server metadata, permissions failures, and database-qualified table names need the DB runtime harness.
This fixture covers seed traces from the upstream db tests. Full upstream wpdb PHPUnit parity remains a domain-level closure requirement.
